empty.buildingDesc: This feature will be available in a later phase

login.headline: Unified protection for assets, workloads, containers and runtime
login.subtitle: Asset visibility, vulnerability management, baseline compliance, container security, EDR threat detection and audit — all in one platform.
login.consoleDesc: Enter your account to access the security operations platform

header.pwdChanged: Password changed, please log in again

about.title: About
about.allow1: Source fully open; free to use, modify and distribute
about.allow2: No limits on agent count, user count or data storage
about.allow3: All security capabilities open: baseline, alerts, file integrity, container security, antivirus, vulnerability management, threat detection, auto-response, etc.
about.allow4: Modifications and derivative works must be open-sourced under the same AGPL-3.0 license, retaining copyright notices
about.allow5: When provided as a network service, the complete source must be made available to users
about.deny1: Closed-source commercial use (reselling, hosted services, integration into commercial products, etc.) requires a commercial license

migration.connectHint: Provide the URL and admin account of the MVP1 open-source edition. This tool migrates data read-only via HTTP API and does not modify the old platform.
migration.scopeWarning: Conflict handling: existing records will be skipped. User passwords are not migrated and must be reset by an admin afterwards.
migration.willMigrate: The following tables will be migrated:
migration.executeHint: Once started, the task runs in the background. Track progress in the task list below (refresh the page to update status).

taskReport.emptyDataDesc: This report type has not been generated yet or failed to load

install.localhostHint: Detected access via localhost. Please enter the actual server IP address.
install.addrHint: HTTP address (port 8080) is used to download packages; gRPC address (port 6751) is used by the Agent to report data.
install.autoInstallHint: Use the one-click install script to auto-detect the system and install the matching version:
install.statusHint: Check the Agent status (output "active (running)" means success):
install.logHint: Check network connectivity (logs containing "connected to server" mean it works):
